---
title: "4.6 Pokemon Object Array Challenge"
subtitle: "Using Text Files"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Array of Pokemon from Input File

This challenge combines the skills learned so far.

You may work in pairs.

Create a class `Pokemon` that has at least 3 attributes found in the Pokemon file, including its `name`, `type1`, and `imagefile`, plus any other attributes from the file that you would like.

Write a constructor and getters for these attributes.

## Challenge Deliverables

### `activecode:: challenge-pokemon-file`

Then:

::: {.tight-list}
- read in the data from the pokemon file
- split each line
- save the data in an array of `Pokemon` objects
- write a `findType` method
- loop through the array to find the Pokemon object with the given name
- print and return its type and print its image
:::

## Pokemon Class Starter

```java
import java.io.*;
import java.util.*;

class Pokemon
{
    // 1. Add at least 3 String attributes of a Pokemon
    //   including name, type1, and imagefile

    // 2. Add a constructor that initializes the attributes of a Pokemon
    //  to the values given as arguments

    // 3. Add getters for the attributes

}
```

## PokemonArray Fields and Constructor

```java
public class PokemonArray
{
    // An array of 152 Pokemon objects
    private Pokemon[] pokemonArray = new Pokemon[152];
    private String filename = "pokemon.csv";

    public PokemonArray() throws IOException
    {
         readFile(); // read in the data file into pokemonArray
    }
```

## readFile Starter

```java
    // 4. Write a method to read in the data file (it may throw an exception).
    // Loop through each row to split it into attributes.
    //     Create a new Pokemon object from the attributes.
    //     and save it into the pokemonArray
    public void readFile()
    {

    }
```

## findType Starter

```java
    // 5. Write a findType method that prints out the type of a Pokemon
    // given its name as an argument.
    // It should loop through the array to find the Pokemon object with the correct name.
    // It should print and return the type and print the image.
    // This method can call printHTMLimage(url) defined below.
    public String findType(String name)
    {
        // Loop through the array to find the Pokemon with the given name
        // Call the getType method to get the type of the Pokemon
        // Call the printHTMLimage method to print out the image
        // return the type


        return "Type";
    }
```

## Image Method and Main

```java
    // This method will just work on Runestone to print out images
    public static void printHTMLimage(String url)
    {
        System.out.print("<img src=" + url + " width=300px />");
    }

    public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException
    {
        PokemonArray obj = new PokemonArray();
        // Call your method to find the type of a Pokemon and display its image
        System.out.println("Pikachu's type is " + obj.findType("Pikachu"));

    }
}
```

## Test Targets

### Runestone checks

The tests check:

::: {.tight-list}
- at least 3 private instance variables
- a `public Pokemon(` constructor
- a call to `.split`
- `findType("Pikachu")` returns `Electric`
- `findType("Bulbasaur")` returns `Grass`
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- create at least three `private` attributes in `Pokemon`
- initialize attributes in a `Pokemon` constructor
- write getters such as `getName()` and `getType()`
- split each CSV row before constructing a `Pokemon`
- store each constructed `Pokemon` object into `pokemonArray`
- use `.equals` when comparing the target name to a Pokemon name
- return the found Pokemon type and print its image
:::

## End

### 4.6 Part 3 complete

Next: wrapper classes.
